title: Pledgeted tissue anchor
abstract: An anchoring system comprises a first anchoring device comprising a first attachment mechanism and a plugging device and a second anchoring device comprising a second attachment mechanism, each of the first anchoring device and the second anchoring device configured to enter a tissue wall at a proximal side of the tissue wall, the first anchoring device being configured to exit the tissue wall through a first hole on a distal side of the tissue wall, the second anchoring device configured to exit the tissue wall at a second hole on the distal side of the tissue wall, the first attachment mechanism configured to attach to the second attachment mechanism outside of the distal side of the tissue wall, and the plugging device configured to cover the first hole and fit between at least a portion of the first anchoring device and the distal side of the tissue wall.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. An anchoring system comprising: a first anchoring device comprising a first attachment mechanism and a plugging device; and a second anchoring device comprising a second attachment mechanism; wherein: each of the first anchoring device and the second anchoring device is configured to enter a tissue wall at a proximal side of the tissue wall; the first anchoring device is configured to exit the tissue wall through a first hole on a distal side of the tissue wall; the second anchoring device is configured to exit the tissue wall at a second hole on the distal side of the tissue wall; the first attachment mechanism is configured to attach to the second attachment mechanism outside of the distal side of the tissue wall; and the plugging device is configured to at least partially cover the first hole; and to fit between at least a portion of the first anchoring device and the distal side of the tissue wall. 2. The anchoring system of claim 1, wherein the first anchoring device comprises a suture, the first attachment mechanism is situated near an end portion of the suture, and the plugging device is attached to the suture. 3. The anchoring system of claim 2, wherein the plugging device is configured to lay flat against the suture while inside the tissue wall and to extend perpendicularly from the suture after exiting the tissue wall. 4. The anchoring system of any of claims 1-3, wherein the plugging device is at least partially composed of felt. 5. The anchoring system of any of claims 1-4, wherein the first anchoring mechanism comprises at least one of a barb, a magnet, a hook, a lasso, a corkscrew, a peg, and a tie. 6. The anchoring system of any of claims 1-5, wherein the first anchoring device is configured to puncture the proximal side of the tissue wall. 7. The anchoring system of any of claims 1-6, wherein the plugging device is configured to fit between at least a portion of the second anchoring device and the distal side of the tissue wall. 8. The anchoring system of claim 7, wherein the plugging device is configured to at least partially cover the second hole. 9. An anchoring system comprising: a first anchoring device comprising a first attachment mechanism; and a second anchoring device comprising a second attachment mechanism; wherein: each of the first anchoring device and the second anchoring device is configured to enter a tissue wall at a proximal side of the tissue wall; at least a portion of the first anchoring device is configured to exit the tissue wall through a first hole on a distal side of the tissue wall; 

 at least a portion of the second anchoring device is configured to exit the tissue wall through a second hole on the distal side of the tissue; the first attachment mechanism is configured to attach to the second attachment mechanism outside of the distal side of the tissue wall; and the second attachment mechanism is configured to at least partially cover the first hole to prevent bleeding. 10. The anchoring system of claim 9, wherein the second attachment mechanism is further configured to fit between at least a portion of the first anchoring device and the distal side of the tissue wall. 11. The anchoring system of claim 9 or claim 8, wherein the second attachment mechanism is at least partially composed of metal and cloth. 12. The anchoring system of any of claims 9-11, wherein the first attachment mechanism comprises a corkscrew or a barb. 13. The anchoring system of any of claims 9-12, further comprising a third anchoring device comprising a third attachment mechanism, wherein the third anchoring device is configured to enter the tissue wall at the proximal side of the tissue wall, at least a portion of the third anchoring device is configured to exit the tissue wall through a third hole on the distal side of the tissue wall, and the third attachment mechanism is configured to attach to the second attachment mechanism outside of the distal side of the tissue wall. 14. The anchoring system of claim 13, wherein the second attachment mechanism is configured to at least partially cover the third hole to prevent bleeding. 15. The anchoring system of claim 13 or claim 14, wherein the third attachment mechanism comprises a corkscrew or a barb. 16. A method for anchoring to a tissue wall, said method comprising: delivering a catheter into close proximity of the tissue wall; passing a first anchoring device through the catheter, the first anchoring device comprising a first attachment mechanism; passing a second anchoring device through the catheter, the second anchoring device comprising a second attachment mechanism; inserting the first anchoring device into a proximal side of the tissue wall such that at least a portion of the first attachment mechanism protrudes from a distal side of the tissue wall; inserting the second anchoring device into the proximal side of the tissue wall such that at least a portion of the second attachment mechanism protrudes from the distal side of the tissue wall; and connecting the first attachment mechanism to the second attachment mechanism outside the distal side of the tissue wall. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the first anchoring device is configured to protrude from a first hole in the distal side of the tissue wall, and the second attachment mechanism is configured to at least partially cover the first hole to prevent bleeding. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein the second attachment mechanism is configured to fit between at least a portion of the first anchoring device and the distal side of the tissue wall. 19. The method of any of claims 16-18, wherein the first anchoring device further comprises a plugging device, the first anchoring device is configured to protrude from a first hole in the distal side of the tissue wall, and the plugging device is configured to at least partially cover the first hole to prevent bleeding. 20. The method of any of claims 16-19, wherein the first attachment mechanism comprises a corkscrew or a barb.
